note: Scenario contract for the card-rewards Google Calendar delivery feature. The three canonical behaviours (SCN-097-A01 write+idempotent, A02 disabled->Web-UI-only, A03 enabled-but-misconfigured fail-loud) are unit-proven under SCOPE-01. SCN-097-A01 is ALSO tracked under SCOPE-02 as the live self-hosted delivery proof (same behaviour, second delivery proof: a real event written to the operator's Google Calendar with idempotent no-duplicate re-sync). Every scenario is linked 1:1 to its owning scope, an existing Go test, and a report.md evidence anchor.

scenarios.title: Monthly recommendation is written to Google Calendar
scenarios.given: card_rewards.enabled and calendar_sync are true with a valid credential + calendar id
scenarios.when: the card-rewards calendar sync runs for a period
scenarios.then: each recommendation with a recommended card is written as a stable-UID Google Calendar event, and a re-run updates the same event without duplicating it

scenarios.title: Calendar sync disabled keeps recommendations in the Web UI only
scenarios.then: no calendar client is constructed, no event is written, and the recommendations remain queryable for the Web UI

scenarios.title: Enabled-but-misconfigured fails loud
scenarios.given: card_rewards.enabled and calendar_sync are true but CARD_REWARDS_GCAL_CREDENTIALS or CARD_REWARDS_CALENDAR_ID is empty
scenarios.then: config load fails naming the offending key

scenarios.title: Monthly recommendation is written to Google Calendar (live self-hosted proof)
scenarios.given: the new signed image is deployed on the self-hosted host with calendar_sync true and the operator's real Google OAuth credential
scenarios.when: the deployed GoogleCalendarClient runs a card-rewards calendar sync
scenarios.then: a real card-rewards event is written to the operator's Google Calendar with the stable UID; a re-run updates the same event without duplicating it; a delete removes it
